content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand the project context and then audit the four Lean files.
I'll read the directive, project context, and then audit each of the four Lean files systematically.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.

129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.

152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)

154	These run automatically between phases:

156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.

28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — finite affine covers with quasi-compact overlaps -/

37	theorem Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact (X : Scheme.{u})
38	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] :
39	    ∃ s : Set X.affineOpens, s.Finite ∧ (⨆ i ∈ s, (i : X.Opens)) = ⊤ ∧
40	      ∀ U ∈ s, ∀ V ∈ s, IsCompact ((U : Set X) ∩ (V : Set X)) := by
41	  obtain ⟨s, hs, he⟩ :=
42	    (isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mp
43	      (by simpa using isCompact_univ (X := ↥X))
44	  refine ⟨s, hs, he.symm, ?_⟩
45	  intro U _ V _
46	  exact quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens.mp ‹_› U V
47

49	
50	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in
51	/-- For `M : X.Modules` and any open cover `U : ι → X.Opens`, the sheaf-condition fork
52	of the underlying abelian-group presheaf of `M` is a limit (an equalizer of products):
53	```

55	```

65	    {ι : Type u} (U : ι → X.Opens) :
66	    IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U) :=
67	  ((isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts M.presheaf).mp M.isSheaf U).some
68	
69	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in

80	    ∃ (ι : Type u) (_ : Finite ι) (U : ι → X.Opens),

88	  · rw [← hcov, iSup_subtype']

10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes

18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.

39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",

55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.

69	```

74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57	
58	## Mathlib status
59

98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.

229	theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
230	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
231	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
232	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
233	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
234	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
235	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
236	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
237	  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
238	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
239	  exact IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ hmul
240	
241	/-- The **transition map** `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`def:gr_transition`):
242	the away-localisation lift of the pre-hom `θ̃_{I,J}` along `P^J_I`, available because
243	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is a unit (`isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`). It is the comorphism of
244	the chart-overlap isomorphism `U^I_J → U^J_I`. Project-local. -/
245	noncomputable def transitionMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
246	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
247	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
248	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
249	    (g := (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
250	    (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
251	
252	/-- `θ_{I,I}` is the identity (`lem:gr_transition_self`): since `P^I_I = 1` the minor
253	`X^I_I = 1` is its own Cramer inverse, so the pre-hom is the structure map and its
254	away-localisation lift is the identity ring homomorphism. Project-local. -/
255	theorem transitionMap_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
256	    transitionMap d r I I hI hI = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
257	  have hmin1 : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
258	    simp only [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
259	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
260	  have hinv1 : universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
261	    simp only [universalMinorInv, hmin1]
262	    exact inv_one
263	  have himg : imageMatrix d r I I hI hI = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
264	    rw [imageMatrix, hinv1]
265	    exact Matrix.one_mul _
266	  have hpre : (transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI).toRingHom
267	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
268	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
269	    apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
270	    · intro n
271	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.C n) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.C n)
272	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_C,
273	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
274	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)), MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
275	    · intro e
276	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.X e) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.X e)
277	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, himg, Matrix.map_apply]
278	      congr 1
279	      rw [universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2]
280	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I I hI hI))
281	  simp only [transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.id_comp]
282	  exact hpre

128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.

143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/

233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
235	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
236	predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local

239	namespace SheafOfModules
240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules

335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is

341	annihilator commutes with localization:

343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.

346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
420	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
421	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
422	
423	end Module
424	
425	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
426	
427	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
428	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
429	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
430	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
431	
432	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
433	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
434	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
435	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
436	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
437	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
438	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
439	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
440	
441	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
442	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
443	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
444	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
445	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
446	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
447	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
448	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
449	
450	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
451	
452	open CategoryTheory Limits
453	
454	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
455	
456	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
457	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
458	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
459	
460	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
461	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
462	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
463	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
464	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
465	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
466	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
467	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
468	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
469	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
470	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
471	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
472	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
473	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
474	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
475	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
476	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
477	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
478	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
479	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
480	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
481	  rw [key] at hinst0
482	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
483	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
484	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
485	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
486	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
487	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
488	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
489	  rw [he] at h2
490	  exact h2
491	
492	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
493	
494	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
495	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
496	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
497	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
498	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
499	
500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	/-- A morphism of sheaves of `R`-modules on `Spec R` that is an isomorphism on every basic open
549	`D(f)` is an isomorphism. This is the "isomorphism on a basis ⟹ isomorphism" reduction specialised

600	
601	/-- **Cocycle condition** (`lem:gr_cocycle`): over the triple overlap, the transition maps satisfy
602	`Θ_{I,K} = Θ_{I,J} ∘ Θ_{J,K}` as ring homs `S_K →+* S_I`. Together with `θ_{I,I} = id`
603	(`transitionMap_self`) this is the gluing datum for the Grassmannian charts. -/
604	theorem cocycleCondition (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
605	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
606	    cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK
607	      = (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp (cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK) := by
608	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
609	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ))
610	  have hIK : (cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
611	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
612	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
613	      = (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
614	          (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom := by
615	    rw [cocycleΘIK]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
616	  have hJK : (cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
617	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
618	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
619	      = (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
620	          (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom := by
621	    rw [cocycleΘJK]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
622	  rw [hIK, RingHom.comp_assoc, hJK]
623	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
624	  · intro n
625	    exact RingHom.congr_fun (RingHom.ext_int
626	      (((awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
627	        (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom).comp MvPolynomial.C)
628	      (((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
629	        ((awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
630	          (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom)).comp MvPolynomial.C)) n
631	  · intro e
632	    have h := congrFun (congrFun (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq d r I J K hI hJ hK) e.1) e.2.1
633	    simpa [Matrix.map_apply, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, RingHom.comp_apply] using h
634	
635	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level charts and the glue data
636	
637	We assemble the affine charts (`affineChart`), their principal-open overlaps
638	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`, the transition isomorphisms (`transitionMap`), and
639	the cocycle (`cocycleCondition`) into the data of
640	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, whose `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme
641	`Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
642	
643	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
644	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
645	
646	/-- `P^I_I = 1`: the `I`-minor determinant of the universal matrix `X^I` is the
647	unit `1`, since `X^I_I` is the identity (`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`).
648	Project-local. -/
649	theorem minorDet_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
650	    minorDet d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
651	  rw [minorDet, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.det_one]
652	
653	/-- The principal-open overlap `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` as a scheme: the affine
654	spectrum of the away-localisation of the chart ring `R^I` at the minor determinant
655	`P^I_J`. Project-local: the `V`-object of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
656	noncomputable def chartOverlap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
657	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Scheme :=
658	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
659	
660	/-- The canonical open immersion `U^I_J → U^I` of the principal open into the chart,
661	the comorphism of the structure map `R^I → R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local: the `f`-field
662	of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
663	noncomputable def chartIncl (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
664	    (hJ : J.card = d) : chartOverlap d r I J hI hJ ⟶ affineChart d r I :=
665	  Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
666	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
667	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
668	
669	instance chartIncl_isOpenImmersion (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
670	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsOpenImmersion (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) :=
671	  inferInstanceAs (IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
672	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
673	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))))
674	
675	/-- The self-inclusion `U^I_I → U^I` is an isomorphism: since `P^I_I = 1`
676	(`minorDet_self`) the away-localisation is the identity, so its `Spec` is an iso.
677	Project-local: the `f_id`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
678	theorem chartIncl_self_isIso (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
679	    IsIso (chartIncl d r I I hI hI) := by
680	  have hx : IsUnit (minorDet d r I I hI hI) := by rw [minorDet_self]; exact isUnit_one
681	  have e : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ ≃ₐ[_]
682	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI) :=
683	    IsLocalization.atUnit _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) _ hx
684	  have hbij : Function.Bijective
685	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
686	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI))) := by
687	    have hfun : (⇑(algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
688	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))) = ⇑e := by
689	      funext y; simp [← e.commutes y]
690	    rw [hfun]; exact e.bijective
691	  have : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
692	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
693	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))) :=
694	    (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mpr hbij
695	  change IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
696	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
697	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))))
698	  infer_instance
699	
700	/-- The scheme-level transition `U^I_J → U^J_I`, the comorphism (`Spec.map`) of the
701	ring transition map `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local: the
702	`t`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
703	noncomputable def chartTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
704	    (hJ : J.card = d) : chartOverlap d r I J hI hJ ⟶ chartOverlap d r J I hJ hI :=
705	  Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ))
706	
707	/-- `t_{I,I} = id`: the self-transition is the identity, from `transitionMap_self`.
708	Project-local: the `t_id`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
709	theorem chartTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
710	    chartTransition d r I I hI hI
711	      = CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id (chartOverlap d r I I hI hI) := by
712	  rw [chartTransition, transitionMap_self, CommRingCat.ofHom_id, Spec.map_id]
713	  rfl
714	
715	/-- The pullback of two principal-open inclusions
716	`Spec R[1/x] → Spec R ← Spec R[1/y]` is `Spec R[1/(xy)]`: combine `pullbackSpecIso`
717	(the pullback is `Spec` of the tensor product `R[1/x] ⊗_R R[1/y]`) with the
718	localisation identification `R[1/x] ⊗_R R[1/y] ≅ R[1/(xy)]`
719	(`IsLocalization.Away.mul'`, `IsLocalization.algEquiv`). Project-local helper for the
720	triple-overlap pullbacks of the Grassmannian glue data; stated over a general base ring
721	so its proof term carries the needed `IsScalarTower` instances (avoiding a typeclass
722	timeout over the heavy chart ring). -/
723	noncomputable def awayPullbackIso {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
724	    Limits.pullback
725	        (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away x))))
726	        (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y)))) ≅
727	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (x * y))) :=
728	  letI : IsLocalization.Away (x * y)
729	      (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) :=
730	    IsLocalization.Away.mul' (Localization.Away x) _ x y
731	  (pullbackSpecIso A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) ≪≫
732	    Scheme.Spec.mapIso
733	      ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
734	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
735	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).symm.op
736	
737	/-- The first leg of `awayPullbackIso` is the left away-inclusion: under the
738	identification `pullback ≅ Spec R[1/(xy)]`, the projection to `Spec R[1/x]` is
739	`Spec.map` of `awayInclLeft x y`. Project-local: the `pullback.fst`-compatibility
740	needed for the `t_fac` field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
741	theorem awayPullbackIso_inv_fst {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
742	    (awayPullbackIso x y).inv ≫
743	        Limits.pullback.fst
744	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away x))))
745	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y))))
746	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayInclLeft x y)) := by
747	  letI : IsLocalization.Away (x * y)
748	      (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) :=
749	    IsLocalization.Away.mul' (Localization.Away x) _ x y
750	  rw [awayPullbackIso, Iso.trans_inv, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst,
751	    show (Scheme.Spec.mapIso ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
752	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
753	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).symm.op).inv
754	      = Spec.map ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
755	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
756	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).hom from rfl,
757	    ← Spec.map_comp]
758	  congr 1
759	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
760	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, RingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso_hom]
761	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers x)

832	away-pullback identification, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`, and the

834	noncomputable def chartTransition' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

836	    Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ⟶

839	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫

847	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :

884	theorem chartTransition'_fac (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

920	The `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data reduces (after stripping the conjugating

935	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :

944	private lemma rotMid (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

959	Both reduce to `W = X^I` over `S_I` via the `(W_K)⁻¹ W` computation, using `W_I = 1`.

961	private lemma transitionInvImageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

967	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
968	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) := by
969	  set incl := (cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
970	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)) with hincldef
971	  have hcomp : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
972	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI)))

554	private theorem isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
555	    {F G : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} R) (Spec R)} (α : F ⟶ G)
556	    (h : ∀ f : R, IsIso (α.1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) : IsIso α := by
557	  have hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _)) :=
558	    PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
559	  have hinj : ∀ U ∈ Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _),
560	      Function.Injective (α.1.app (.op U)) := by
561	    rintro U ⟨f, rfl⟩
562	    exact ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).1
563	  have hstalk : ∀ x, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor (ModuleCat.{u} R) x).map α.1) := by
564	    intro x
565	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
566	    refine ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB hinj x, ?_⟩
567	    intro t
568	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hUB, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB G.presheaf x t
569	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := hUB
570	    obtain ⟨s', rfl⟩ := ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).2 s
571	    exact ⟨F.presheaf.germ _ x hxU s', by rw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]⟩
572	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso α

574	/-- A linear map intertwining two localizations of the same module at the same submonoid is
575	bijective: if `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : M →ₗ M''` both exhibit a localization at `S` and
576	`h : M' →ₗ M''` satisfies `h ∘ₗ f = g`, then `h` is bijective (it is the canonical localization
577	isomorphism `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`). Stated with the two `IsLocalizedModule` facts as
578	explicit hypotheses to avoid typeclass-diamond ambiguity at the call site. -/
579	private theorem bijective_comp_of_localizations {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
580	    {M M' M'' : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
581	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module R M''] {f : M →ₗ[R] M'} {g : M →ₗ[R] M''} {h : M' →ₗ[R] M''}
582	    (hf : IsLocalizedModule S f) (hg : IsLocalizedModule S g) (hh : h ∘ₗ f = g) :
583	    Function.Bijective h := by
584	  haveI := hf; haveI := hg
585	  have heq : h = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).toLinearMap := by
586	    apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext S (f := f) (f' := g)
587	    apply LinearMap.ext
588	    intro x
589	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, ← LinearMap.comp_apply, hh,
590	      LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
591	  rw [heq]
592	  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).bijective
593	
594	/-- **`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` from per-basic-open section localization** (the cheap stalk/section
595	assembly of `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the blueprint "G1-assemble" step). If for every
596	`f : R` the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` of a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R`
597	exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R` — exactly the conclusion of G1-core
598	(`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`,
599	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
600	an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).
601	
602	On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
603	two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
604	instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
605	localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
606	the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
607	`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
608	`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.
609

611	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is

1229	structure-map image of `R^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (right
1230	factor), which together generate `R^I_J = R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local. -/
1231	theorem diagonalRingMap_surjective (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1232	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Function.Surjective (diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
1233	  intro z
1234	  -- `z · uⁿ = algebraMap a` for some `a` and `n = ` power of `P^I_J`, by the localisation
1235	  -- property, where `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1236	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) z
1237	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1238	  -- The witness pushes the power into the second tensor factor: `a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ`, avoiding
1239	  -- any arithmetic in the tensor-product ring.
1240	  refine ⟨a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (minorDet d r J I hJ hI ^ n), ?_⟩
1241	  -- `v := θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is the inverse of `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1242	  have hvu : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1243	      algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1244	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 :=
1245	    transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul d r I J hI hJ
1246	  -- `algebraMap ↑s = uⁿ`.
1247	  have hsu : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1248	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (s : _)
1249	      = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1250	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1251	    rw [← hn, map_pow]
1252	  -- `algebraMap a = z · uⁿ`.
1253	  have key : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1254	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a
1255	      = z * (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1256	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1257	    rw [← hs, hsu]
1258	  -- `δ(a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ) = algebraMap a · vⁿ = z · uⁿ · vⁿ = z · (u·v)ⁿ = z`.
1259	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_pow,
1260	    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', key, mul_assoc, ← mul_pow, mul_comm _
1261	      (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)), hvu, one_pow, mul_one]
1262	
1263	/-- The intersection pullback `U^I ∩ U^J = pullback (ι i) (ι j)` is the chart overlap
1264	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`lem:gr_separated`, source identification): from the glue-data
1265	universal property `theGlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit`, whose cone point is `V (i,j) = chartOverlap`.
1266	This is the `e₂` source iso of the restricted-diagonal closed-immersion argument. Project-local. -/
1267	noncomputable def pullbackιIso (d r : ℕ) (i j : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1268	    Limits.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι i) ((theGlueData d r).ι j) ≅
1269	      chartOverlap d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 :=
1270	  (Limits.limit.isLimit _).conePointUniqueUpToIso
1271	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j)
1272

1274

1278	of `scheme ×_⊤ scheme`, to showing that on each patch `U^I ×_⊤ U^J` the restricted diagonal is a
1279	closed immersion. Exactly as in `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.isSeparated`

751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence
